title: phlegmariurus lockyeri
intro: Phlegmariurus lockyeri, also known as Lockyer's climbing fork, is a type of plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family, a group of small, non-vascular plants commonly referred to as club mosses. Traditionally, Phlegmariurus lockyeri has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the respiratory system. Phlegmariurus lockyeri is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the dried plant material being steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea or used as a topical poultice to promote healing and reduce swelling.
